title: Ascocentrum aurantiacum
intro: Ascocentrum aurantiacum, also known as the Golden Orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ascocentrum aurantiacum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote overall health and well-being.
